destinations.evidence: Shared helpers implement the required max-block formula, zero-call behavior, and u32 saturation.
Verification uses the shared u64 helper for full-width scheduled call expiry.
Builder consumers share the same scheduled-verification math surface rather than duplicating arithmetic.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in port shared scheduled-verification helpers and builder consumers.

related_but_insufficient_code: Old crates/scheduling singleton is absent; current shared formula lives in ritual-scheduled-verification.

rationale: The candidate introduces shared max-block helpers and rewires verification, block-building, and hydration consumers to the same arithmetic.
statement: Scheduled call max-block derivation uses start_block + frequency * (num_calls - 1) + ttl, returns 0 when num_calls is 0, preserves full u64 values for verification, and uses the shared saturating u32 helper for builder and hydration consumers.
suggested_falsification_tests: Compare ScheduledCall::expiry_block, scheduled block-building max block, and hydration max_block_number for normal, zero-call, and overflow-sized inputs; assert all use the shared formula and u32 consumers saturate to u32::MAX.
summary: Scheduled max block uses one shared formula in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Compare helper outputs for normal, zero-call, and overflow-sized inputs; u64 verification should preserve full value and u32 consumers should saturate.
title: Scheduled max block uses one shared formula
